multiplechoiceresponse.label: In this lecture, what does achieving demographic parity at level ε mean?

choice.div: The absolute pass-rate gap between the groups is less than ε (e.g., 1%).
Pass-rate difference between the groups is at least ε.
Protected attributes (e.g., race, gender) are ignored during training.
The two groups’ pass rates must be exactly equal (ε = 0).

div.p: The lecture defines demographic parity as the absolute difference in positive outcomes between groups being below a small threshold ε (e.g., 1%), not “at least ε”, not merely ignoring protected attributes, and not requiring exact equality.
